docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: We welcome contributions to the AIR Plugin! Here's how you can help:

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Clone your fork
  3. Install dependencies
  4. Create a branch
  5. Make changes
  6. Submit PR

docs.title: Guidelines
docs.text:
• Follow PEP 8 style guide
  • Write tests for new features
  • Update documentation
  • Keep commits atomic
  • Make sure the pipeline checks pass

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
Always make sure to run the tests after your changes. Only push code which passes all of the tests. 

# Running the tests
pytest

If you create new code which does not have any tests, create the tests for your components by placing your tests file in the tests folder. The name of your test file should be test_thenameofthecomponentyoucreated.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide will help you get started with the AIR Plugin quickly and efficiently.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10 or higher
  • pip package manager

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: You can find a comprehensive installation guide here: Installation

docs.title: Contributing to the Documentation
docs.text:
First install the necessary packages to use mkdoc 

pip install -e .[docs]
Afterwards you can add or modify the contents of the docs folder to create or change the current documentation.

If you would like to see the changes made to the document locally use 

mkdocs serve

mkdocs gh-deploy # Only run this when everything is finalized
# because this pushes the documentation to the website

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text:
• Operating System: Windows 10+, macOS 10.15+, or Linux
  • Python: Version 3.10 or higher

docs.title: Build From Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/johk3/AIR.git
cd AIR

# Optional(if you already use conda or something else)
# Setup the virtual environment
python -m venv .venv
# Activate the virtual environment
source .venv/bin/activate


# Install all required packages for development
pip install -e .[dev]

Add all the necessary API keys and parameters to the .env file by copying the .env.example to .env and then changing .env. 

cp .env.example .env

Afterwards you can run the program with 

python main.py
